# Generated by ollama (codellama)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.95.0/http/server.ts";
import { parse } from "https://deno.land/std@0.95.0/flags/mod.ts";

const PORT = 3000;
const MODULE_PATHS = [""];

serve(async (req, res) => {
  const { method, url } = req;

  if (method === "GET" && url.pathname === "/") {
    // Handle index route
    res.statusCode = 200;
    res.setHeader("Content-Type", "text/html");
    res.end("<h1>Welcome to my application</h1>");
  } else if (method === "GET" && url.pathname.startsWith("/modules")) {
    // Handle module route
    const [moduleName] = url.pathname.split("/").slice(-1)[0];
    if (!MODULE_PATHS.includes(moduleName)) {
      res.statusCode = 404;
      res.end(`Module not found: ${moduleName}`);
    } else {
      const modulePath = MODULE_PATHS[moduleName];
      try {
        // Import module dynamically using dynamic import()
        const module = await import(modulePath);
        res.statusCode = 200;
        res.setHeader("Content-Type", "application/json");
        res.end(JSON.stringify({ status: "success", message: `Module ${moduleName} loaded successfully` }));
      } catch (error) {
        console.error(error);
        res.statusCode = 500;
        res.setHeader("Content-Type", "text/plain");
        res.end(`Error loading module: ${error.message}`);
      }
    }
  } else {
    // Handle unknown routes
    res.statusCode = 404;
    res.end("Not found");
  }
});
